subject: Geography
title: The Northwest Passage
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Northwest Passage.

hook.text: For centuries, explorers searched for a sea route through the Arctic from Atlantic to Pacific.

buildup.text: Franklin's 1845 expedition vanished in the ice. All 129 men died—the worst Arctic disaster ever.

discovery.text: Amundsen finally navigated the passage in 1906, taking three years in a tiny fishing vessel.

twist.text: Today, melting sea ice has opened the passage to commercial shipping for the first time.

climax.text: The route that killed hundreds of explorers is now opening because the planet is warming.

punchline.text: The passage that couldn't be conquered was opened by climate change.

quiz.question: Who first successfully navigated the Northwest Passage?
